content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 18, 1987

Carl A. Gosline
27280 Bear Terrace
Willits, CA 95490

Re: Your Request for Informal
Assistance, Our File No.
I-87-306

Dear Mr. Gosline:

You have requested general information regarding three
topics: (1) organization of political campaigning, (2) regulation
of the political activities of local agency public employees, and
(3) freedom of information provisions in California laws.

The Commission's authority is limited to interpretation of the
Political Reform Act. (Government Code Sections 81000-91015.)
The last two topics are not covered by the Political Reform Act.
Therefore, the Commission does not have jurisdiction to interpret
these laws. A city attorney, county counsel or district attorney
would be the most appropriate person to contact for interpretation
of laws and regulations governing local public employees and
freedom of information requirements.

The organization of political campaigns is regulated by the
Political Reform Act only to the extent that campaign disclosure
statements may be required for persons who spend money for
political purposes. Enclosed is a manual on campaign disclosure
requirements. It should answer your questions regarding the
specific requirements.

If you have further questions, you may write to me or call
(916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

By: Bruce W. Robeck
Political Reform Consultant

DMG:BWR:kmt

Enclosure

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

L. BARRY MACK

BARRY MACK                          A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION                    AREA CODE 619
DONALD E. SMITH                     300 EAST TAHQUITZ M C CALLUM WAY                   322-5656
                                    SUITE 105
                                    PALM SPRINGS, CALIFORNIA 92262-6982


                                    December 1, 1987


Fair Political Practices Commission
State of California
428 "J" Street, Ste. 800
Sacramento, CA  95814

ATTN:  Katheryne Donovan, Counsel
       Legal Division

       Re:  MARYANOV, et al. adv. JONES
            Your File:  I-87-252

Dear Ms. Donovan:

     This office represents Susan Marx, Minna Maryanov, and
Michael McCabe as individuals and as members of the Board of
Education in the above-captioned matter.

     You are in receipt of a letter dated September 28, 1987
from Neil G. McNiece of the law firm Haidin Dickson, Brown &
Bonesteel which enclosed a copy of plaintiff's Second Amended and
Supplemental Complaint, a brief outline of the facts surrounding
the dispute, and a copy of the unpublished Appellate decision
regarding Ms. Jones' Petition for Writ of Mandate.

     By letter dated October 14, 1987, you responded to Mr.
McNiece indicating that you were unable to grant his request
contained in the above-referenced letter since he did not appear
to be authorized by the officials whose duties under the Act are
in question.  Our firm represents the three individual Board
members and has authority to request on their behalf that the Fair
Political Practices Commission issue an opinion pursuant to
Government Code Section 8314 stating how the Board should proceed
in setting the duties and obligations of Marx, Maryanov, and
McCabe.

     This case is currently set for trial on March 14, 1988
in the Indio Branch of the Riverside Superior Court.  If any
further information or documentation regarding any aspect of this
litigation will aid you in advising the individual Board members,
please immediately notify the undersigned.

(cont.)

December 1, 1987
Katheryne Donovan, Counsel
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
-Page Two-


It is requested that the Commission provide copies of
its opinion to our firm, Mr. McNiece, and Darryl A. De Cuir of
Chase, Rotchford, Drukker & Bogust at 600 N. Arrowhead Avenue,
Suite 201, San Bernardino, CA 92401.

Very truly yours,

L. BARRY MACK,
A Professional Law Corporation

BY: [illegible]
DONALD E. SMITH

DES:jar

CC: Susan Marx
Minna Maryanov
Lt. Michael McCabe
Darryl A. De Cuir, Esq.
Neil G. McNiece, Esq.

December 8, 1987


Carl A. Gosline
27280 Bear Terrace
Willis, Ca  95490

                                                Re:  87-306

Dear Mr. Gosline:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on December 4, 1987 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission.  If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice.  If more information is needed, the person assigned to
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to
advise you as to the information needed.  If your request is
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we
can.  (See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec.
18329).)

     You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [illegible] [illegible]

                                        Jeanne Pritchard
                                        Chief
                                        Technical Assistance and Analysis
                                        Division

JP:plh


428 I Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

CARL A. GOSLINE

27280 Bear Terrace • Willits, California 95490
(707) 459-3110

Nov 30, 1987

DEC 4 5:00PM '87

Mr John M. Larson
Chairman, Fair Political
Practices Commission
State office - 428 S Street
Sacramento Ca 95814

Dear Mr Larson

I am interested in certain information relating to
the organization of political campaigns and the statutes
rules regarding the conduct of public employees (non federal),
specifically locally elected officials of community service
districts and general cities as well as the employees of
counties, cities, and community service districts.

From past information sessions by FPPC staff
members, I'm aware that there are details, waiting
procedures to be observed. However, I need an update
by way of anticipating support of candidates in 1988.

Regrettably, I have no information about the
conduct of public employees, and am reminded of
the Hatch Act at the federal level. Please send
whether guidelines - rules, procedures - dos/don'ts
that are available.

In this some context, in there the equivalent
of the Federal Freedom of information act ? If so send
the pertinent facts.

In advance, thanks for your help.

Sincerely
C.A. Gosline

sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asked for general information on three topics: (1) organization of political campaigns, (2) regulation of political activities of local agency public employees, and (3) freedom of information provisions in California laws.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC has limited jurisdiction to the Political Reform Act and cannot interpret laws regarding local public employee regulations or freedom of information. The Commission can address campaign disclosure requirements under the Political Reform Act and enclosed a manual on this topic.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document appears to be a collection of multiple letters (main response dated December 18, 1987, preliminary acknowledgment dated December 8, 1987, and unrelated correspondence). No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present in traditional format. Extracted information from the primary FPPC response letter (Diane M. Griffiths/Bruce W. Robeck).

embedding.summary: This collection includes an FPPC response to Carl Gosline's informal advice request regarding political campaigns, employee regulations, and freedom of information. The FPPC clarifies its jurisdictional limitations and directs the requestor to appropriate local authorities for non-Political Reform Act matters.
